contentBlocks.original:
श्री राम राम रामेति रमे रामे मनोरमे
सहस्रनाम तत् तुल्यं रामनाम वरानने
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrī rāma rāma rāmeti rame rāme manorame
sahasranāma tat tulyaṃ rāmanāma varānane
contentBlocks.translation:
Chanting 'Sri Rama, Rama, Rama,' captivates the mind, filling it with delight and devotion.
O beautiful-faced one, the single name 'Rama' is equivalent to the thousand names (sahasranāma) of Lord Vishnu.
contentBlocks.meanings: श्री (śrī) – auspicious; राम (rāma) – Rama; राम (rāma) – Rama; राम (rāma) – Rama; इति (iti) – thus; रमे (rame) – delight; रामे (rāme) – in Rama; मनोरमे (manorame) – captivating mind (manaḥ:mind | rame:captivating); सहस्रनाम (sahasranāma) – thousand names (sahasra:thousand | nāma:names); तत् (tat) – that; तुल्यं (tulyaṃ) – equivalent; रामनाम (rāmanāma) – name of Rama (rāma:Rama | nāma:name); वरानने (varānane) – O beautiful-faced one (vara:beautiful | ānane:faced one);
